title: Sounding Chains
desc: The hanging chains in the watch bay are meant to tell the difference between ordinary tremor and movement that should not be there.

steps.text:
Just inside the watch bay, three lengths of chain hang from the ceiling over marked stone plates. They are there to react before a person does. Ordinary tremor shifts them one way. Passing draft another. Anything walking deeper in the pillar, if anything does, tends to show itself in patterns that make the watch crew go very still and start using formal voices. Today the outer chain is wrapped half a turn around itself and the center plate has gone dust-blind.

The watch sergeant holds up a little plate brush. "Free the chain line if you want the hanging gear," she says. "Or clean the marked plates below if you prefer the floor. The bay does not have to be dramatic. It simply has to stop misleading me."

options.label: Free and settle the hanging sounding chains
Clean the marked stone plates beneath them

steps.text:
A sounding chain only means anything if it is allowed to hang honestly. You untwist the outer line, steady the middle, and wait through the slow swinging aftermath until each length drops straight above its plate again. Then you test them with a light tap. One answers in a clean metallic whisper. The next in a slightly lower note. The third in the harsh short sound the sergeant says is correct for its weight and never elaborates on.

When the line is finally still, the bay feels less like a rumor and more like an instrument. The sergeant seems pleased by that. "Good. If the pillar intends to tell us something unpleasant, it can at least do so through calibrated chains."

options.label: Step back from the line and leave the chains truthful

steps.text:
The stone plates below the chains carry the dust marks that let the watch crew read motion by pattern instead of nerves. Right now the center plate is obscured by a film of corridor grit, and the outer plate has enough scattered debris to turn any new trace into argument. You brush them both clean, redraw the guide arcs, and leave the surfaces flat and pale enough to catch the next disturbance precisely.

The sergeant splashes a measured tremor from the wall with the butt of her spear and watches the plate take the test mark exactly where she expected. That appears to calm her considerably. "Better. I would rather distrust the pillar for its habits than for my own poor housekeeping."

options.label: Hang the brush away and leave the plates readable
